Blocks.Original: Next.js is a React framework for building full-stack web applications. You use React Components to build user interfaces, and Next.js for additional features and optimizations.
Blocks.Translated: Next.js is a React framework for building full-stack web applications. You use React Components to build user interfaces, and Next.js for additional features and optimizations.

Blocks.Original: It also automatically configures lower-level tools like bundlers and compilers. You can instead focus on building your product and shipping quickly.
Blocks.Translated: It also automatically configures lower-level tools like bundlers and compilers. You can instead focus on building your product and shipping quickly.

Blocks.Original: Whether you're an individual developer or part of a larger team, Next.js can help you build interactive, dynamic, and fast React applications.
Blocks.Translated: Whether you're an individual developer or part of a larger team, Next.js can help you build interactive, dynamic, and fast React applications.

Blocks.Original: The docs are organized into 3 sections:
Blocks.Translated: The docs are organized into 3 sections:

Blocks.Original: Getting Started: Step-by-step tutorials to help you create a new application and learn the core Next.js features.
Blocks.Translated: [Getting Started](docs/app/getting-started.html): Step-by-step tutorials to help you create a new application and learn the core Next.js features.

Blocks.Original: Guides: Tutorials on specific use cases, choose what's relevant to you.
Blocks.Translated: [Guides](docs/app/guides.html): Tutorials on specific use cases, choose what's relevant to you.

Blocks.Original: API Reference: Detailed technical reference for every feature.
Blocks.Translated: [API Reference](docs/app/api-reference.html): Detailed technical reference for every feature.

Blocks.Original: Use the sidebar to navigate through the sections, or search (Ctrl+K or Cmd+K) to quickly find a page.
Blocks.Translated: Use the sidebar to navigate through the sections, or search (Ctrl+K or Cmd+K) to quickly find a page.

Blocks.Original: App Router: The newer router that supports new React features like Server Components.
Blocks.Translated: App Router: The newer router that supports new React features like Server Components.

Blocks.Original: Pages Router: The original router, still supported and being improved.
Blocks.Translated: Pages Router: The original router, still supported and being improved.

Blocks.Original: At the top of the sidebar, you'll notice a dropdown menu that allows you to switch between the App Router and the Pages Router docs.
Blocks.Translated: At the top of the sidebar, you'll notice a dropdown menu that allows you to switch between the [App Router](docs/app.html) and the [Pages Router](docs/pages.html) docs.

Blocks.Original: The App Router and Pages Router handle React versions differently:
Blocks.Translated: The App Router and Pages Router handle React versions differently:شششششششششش

Blocks.Original: App Router: Uses React canary releases built-in, which include all the stable React 19 changes, as well as newer features being validated in frameworks, prior to a new React release.
Blocks.Translated: App Router: Uses [React canary releases](https://react.dev/blog/2023/05/03/react-canaries) built-in, which include all the stable React 19 changes, as well as newer features being validated in frameworks, prior to a new React release.:شششششششششش

Blocks.Original: Pages Router: Uses the React version installed in your project's package.json.
Blocks.Translated: Pages Router: Uses the React version installed in your project's package.json.:شششششششششش

Blocks.Original: This approach ensures new React features work reliably in the App Router while maintaining backwards compatibility for existing Pages Router applications.
Blocks.Translated: This approach ensures new React features work reliably in the App Router while maintaining backwards compatibility for existing Pages Router applications.:شششششششششش

Blocks.Original: Our documentation assumes some familiarity with web development. Before getting started, it'll help if you're comfortable with:
Blocks.Translated: Our documentation assumes some familiarity with web development. Before getting started, it'll help if you're comfortable with:

Blocks.Original: If you're new to React or need a refresher, we recommend starting with our React Foundations course, and the Next.js Foundations course that has you building an application as you learn.
Blocks.Translated: If you're new to React or need a refresher, we recommend starting with our [React Foundations course](learn/react-foundations.html), and the [Next.js Foundations course](learn/dashboard-app.html) that has you building an application as you learn.

Blocks.Original: For the best experience when using a screen reader, we recommend using Firefox and NVDA, or Safari and VoiceOver.
Blocks.Translated: For the best experience when using a screen reader, we recommend using Firefox and NVDA, or Safari and VoiceOver.

Blocks.Original: If you have questions about anything related to Next.js, you're always welcome to ask our community on GitHub Discussions, Discord, X (Twitter), and Reddit.
Blocks.Translated: If you have questions about anything related to Next.js, you're always welcome to ask our community on [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/vercel/next.js/discussions), [Discord](https://discord.com/invite/bUG2bvbtHy), [X (Twitter)](https://x.com/nextjs), and [Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/nextjs).
